RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 233-B, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Sean Pouche, a Republican, represents part of Buchanan and Platte counties (District 13) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2020.
Pouche grew up in Platte County and attended Rockhurst High School in Kansas City. He received a congressional appointment to the US. Merchant Marine Academy (Kings Point) New York, where he earned a Bachelor of Science degree and commission in the U.S. Navy Reserve. In 2010, Pouche was mobilized to Iraq where he served as advisor to U.S. and NATO commanders in Baghdad.
In addition to his legislative duties, Pouche works in the family business (Platte Rental & Supply, Parkville) and has served as a commander in the U.S. Navy Reserve.
Pouche is a member of St. Therese Catholic Parish-Parkville, Knights of Columbus, and the American Legion. He has two children; Joclyn and Will.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Requires publication of a cost estimate and project completion date for any work on the state highway system at the time bidding for a contract on the work first closes
Changes provisions governing the compensation of circuit clerks
Transfers appointment authority of juvenile officers and other juvenile court personnel from the court or the family court administrator to the county prosecuting attorney
Modifies provisions relating to the Kansas City area transportation authority
Creates provisions relating to compensation for services rendered in veterans benefits matters
Establishes the "PRAISE Act" prohibiting the closure of places of worship during an emergency
Modifies provisions relating to access to medical products

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to certain fees collected by the Missouri emergency response commission
Establishes the "Anti-Red Flag Gun Seizure Act"
Establishes the "Anti-Red Flag Gun Seizure Act"
Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Modifies provisions relating to the protection of children
Changes provisions governing the adoption of certain building codes or standards
Modifies provisions relating to the classification of certain residential real property used for short-term rentals
